results.norm: Authors have four obligations when their code is challenged: engage substantively, provide full context, defer on close calls, report investigation findings fully.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete obligation for authors when code is challenged, naming four concrete required actions a reader could follow.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere title or cross-reference; it states the actual content of the obligations.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: Although it enumerates four items, they are stated in full as a single coherent obligation, not merely named as separate norms requiring elaboration.

answers.clean_norm: Authors have four obligations when their code is challenged: engage substantively, provide full context, defer on close calls, report investigation findings fully.

classification.scoring_text: Authors have four obligations when their code is challenged: engage substantively, provide full context, defer on close calls, report investigation findings fully.

results.norm: Consistent review standards across all sections; concerns grounded in code properties, not authorship; blind review where feasible.

N2.reasoning: It is not a single title or identifier pointing to a norm elsewhere; it enumerates substantive content.

N3.reasoning: It contains no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is a semicolon-separated enumeration naming multiple distinct standards (consistent review, code-grounded concerns, blind review) without stating any one in full.

results.norm: An agent who cannot honestly attest must block; silent approval of a known concern is a framework violation.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prescription: an agent who cannot honestly attest must block, and silent approval of a known concern counts as a framework violation, which is applicable as-is.

N2.reasoning: It is a full statement of conduct, not merely a title or cross-reference.

N3.reasoning: It prescribes conduct rather than ratifying or commenting on the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple named norms.

answers.clean_norm: An agent who cannot honestly attest must block; silent approval of a known concern is a framework violation.

results.norm: Approving release is a positive attestation that the agent has no unresolved concern about any part of the codebase and has not concealed a known concern. Silent approval of a known concern is the most serious framework violation possible. An agent who cannot honestly attest must block.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete constitutive standard (what approval attests to) and a prescription (an agent who cannot honestly attest must block), applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: It is full prose stating the norm, not a title or cross-reference pointer.

N3.reasoning: It contains no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single coherent norm about approval rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

results.norm: Patients bear asymmetric risk and cannot speak here; the panel's default posture is protective. When uncertain, treat unresolved concerns as confirmed defects until resolved. Urgency accelerates investigation; it does not lower the evidentiary threshold for approval.

N1.reasoning: The passage states applicable standards of conduct for the panel: adopt a protective default, treat unresolved concerns as confirmed defects when uncertain, and don't lower the evidentiary threshold for urgency—each is a followable prescription.

N2.reasoning: It is full prose stating norms, not a label, heading, or cross-reference.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; it is substantive guidance.

N4.reasoning: Though multiple norms appear, each is stated in full rather than merely enumerated by name.

results.norm: Review is an affirmative obligation, not a reactive one. Each agent must actively review sections outside their own authorship and produce a documented finding for every section reviewed—including what was checked and that no concerns were identified. Silence is not approval.

N1.reasoning: It states a complete prescription: agents must actively review others' sections and produce documented findings, applicable as written.

N2.reasoning: It is a full statement of conduct, not merely a title or pointer.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates one coherent norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: Review is an affirmative obligation, not a reactive one. Each agent must actively review sections outside their own authorship and produce a documented finding for every section reviewed—including what was checked and that no concerns were identified. Silence is not approval.
